title: Avoid Shadowing Class Names in Java
description: In Java, it is problematic to have the same simple class name in different packages, especially when a class name is the same as its superclass or interfaces it implements. This practice, known as class name shadowing, leads to ambiguity and potential bugs as it can be unclear which class is being referred to in the code. It is advised to use unique and descriptive class names that do not conflict with the names of the superclass or interfaces.
recommendation: It is recommended to use unique and descriptive class names that do not conflict with the names of the superclass or interfaces. A good practice is to follow a consistent naming convention across the project.
impact: Using shadowing class names can lead to ambiguity in the code making it hard to understand and maintain, which may result in potential bugs.

checks.message: Field name should not shadow superclass name
